item.title: Two Arrested in Death of Anti-Iranian Regime Activist in Canada
item.description: A man and a woman were charged with murder in what the authorities described as a “targeted incident” against Masood Masjoody, who had gone missing weeks ago in British Columbia.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Urges World to Help Protect Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The president called on Britain, China, France, Japan, and South Korea to send warships to secure the route. Iran has effectively choked off traffic in the narrow waterway, through which a fifth of the world’s oil must transit.

item.title: Severe flooding kills 62 in Kenya, as heavy rains continue
item.description: Eleven people were rescued overnight after a minibus taxi got stuck in rising floodwaters in Nairobi.

item.title: In Scolding the New U.S. Ambassador, South Africa Signals Defiance
item.description: The latest clash between the two countries illustrates the depths to which relations have sunk and the struggle to forge a new path forward.

item.title: Three More Iranian Soccer Players Withdraw Bid for Asylum in Australia
item.description: Seven members of the national women’s team had sought refuge in the country after they were labeled “traitors” at home. Four of them have since changed their minds.

item.title: Iran and the United States: A Long History of Antagonism
item.description: The governments of both countries have repeatedly cast the other as evil, perpetuating a cycle that has culminated in the present war.

item.title: As Rockets Fly Overhead, Residents of Israel’s Border City Stay Underground
item.description: The Israeli government evacuated Kiryat Shmona during the last round of fighting with Hezbollah in 2023. Residents who were told it was safe to return are again under fire.

item.title: Amid War in Iran, Europe’s Flashes of Military Strength Also Show Its Weakness
item.description: To defend allies from Iran, the continent’s powers have mounted a rare show of force. But those efforts have diverted limited resources from other hot spots.

item.title: Here is the latest.
UK must build own nuclear missiles to end reliance on US, say Lib Dems
item.description: Sir Ed Davey is calling on the government to start work on a "fully independent British nuclear deterrent".

item.title: France votes in mayoral elections ahead of 2027 showdown
item.description: Municipal elections will set the scene for next year’s presidential race as the far right looks to gain ground

item.title: ‘Join our people’s army’: Farage sets sights on local election gains
item.description: Reform UK plans high-energy and high-spending campaign in run-up to voting on May 7

item.title: Ukraine peace talks fizzle out as Trump’s focus shifts to Iran
item.description: US president no longer focused on pressuring Vladimir Putin, European officials say

item.title: Military briefing: How mines could tighten Iran’s Gulf chokehold
item.description: US navy appears ill-prepared for potential use of relatively cheap and plentiful weapon in narrow Strait of Hormuz

item.title: ‘Fake workers’ from North Korea use AI to exploit European companies
item.description: Pyongyang’s operatives deploy chatbots to undertake tasks, often in multiple roles

item.title: Trump’s ‘shock and war’ makes this economic crisis different
item.description: Conflict with Iran will leave deeper and more lasting scars than last year’s tariffs crisis

item.title: Wealth taxes’ design flaws consign them to failure
item.description: It may be easier for countries sitting on large deficits to tighten the screws on existing levies

item.title: ‘Firing off 10 to 15 applications a day’: how UK’s hiring slump hits the young
item.description: Ministers’ push to help almost 1mn people not in education, employment or training needs to go further, say analysts

item.title: Renewable jet fuel may face price war with China, warns top producer
item.description: Neste chief calls for a ‘level playing field’ following heavy investment

item.title: A buzz returns to Venezuela’s decayed oil heartland
item.description: Private jets fly over dilapidated Maracaibo as executives seek deals and crude prices surge. A real recovery will not be easy

item.title: Spies and subsidies: China joins Brazil’s $20bn delivery app war
item.description: Local courier group iFood and China-backed Keeta trade claims of corporate espionage and underhand tactics

item.title: Retail traders rush into oil bets as Iran war drives wild price swings
item.description: The biggest US ETF linked to crude prices has seen record inflows as oil market has ‘meme’ moment

item.title: Lithium-ion battery blazes imperil supply chains, warns Los Angeles port chief
item.description: Demand for the storage device is expected to double by 2030 as geopolitical risks pile up

item.title: F.C.C. Chair Threatens to Revoke Broadcasters’ Licenses Over War Coverage
item.description: The comment from Brendan Carr came on the heels of a social media message from President Trump criticizing the news media’s coverage of the war with Iran.

item.title: Trump adds to speculation about Iranian leader’s condition.
Marseille’s Tight Mayoral Race Is a Bellwether for France’s Future
item.description: Voters across France choose mayors this week. The far right is performing strongly in the country’s second city, making the contest there a test of national shifts.

item.title: Iceland’s Chief ‘Lava Cooler’ Is Bracing for the Next Volcanic Eruption
item.description: Helgi Hjorleifsson, a firefighter, is a leader in a national experiment to steer rivers of lava away from important sites. Some called it crazy, but it worked.

item.title: Some Olympic Leaders Want to See Fixed Winter Games Host Cities
item.description: Fewer cities are bidding for Olympics, and those that are can’t always accommodate every event. The Milan-Cortina Games were the most sprawling Olympics in history.

item.title: Police-style powers to tackle fly-tippers being considered
item.description: The move would allow officers to search premises without a warrant, seize assets and arrest those suspected of criminality.

item.title: Japan loses its thirst for vending machines
item.description: Drinks companies cut networks as rising costs and driver shortages undermine business model

item.title: Warning to Americans to leave Iraq is a sign the Iran war is spilling over the border.
Will Trump help or hinder Zimbabwe's white farmers in their compensation battle?
item.description: They have engaged a lobbying firm with close ties to the US president to argue their case.

item.title: Explosion at Jewish School in Amsterdam Is 2nd Antisemitic Attack in 2 Days, Officials Say
item.description: No one was hurt in the explosion at a Jewish school in Amsterdam, or in an earlier one at a Rotterdam synagogue.

item.title: Tehran Seems Largely Empty, as Many Flee and Others Weary of the Fighting
item.description: Residents say a heavy, lifeless atmosphere hangs over the streets of Iran’s capital, which has endured relentless attacks throughout the war.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the U.S.-Israel War With Iran on Saturday
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad was attacked overnight. Israeli strikes continued to hit the capitals of Iran and Lebanon, as Iran refused to relinquish control over a vital oil route.

item.title: US oil groups in line for $63bn windfall from Gulf war disruption
item.description: American producers will be among biggest beneficiaries if crude prices average $100 a barrel this year

item.title: Trump urges UK and other nations to send warships to Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US president says he hopes China, France, Japan and South Korea will also send ships to defend the key oil shipping route.

item.title: Trump puts Japan’s Takaichi under pressure with call to send warships
item.description: US president’s request to deploy navy to the Strait of Hormuz comes as Japanese leader prepares to visit White House

item.title: Middle East war day 15 as it happened: Trump calls on China, UK and Japan to send warships to force open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: London has declined to deploy an aircraft carrier to the Gulf

item.title: Former Labour minister dies after cancer diagnosis
item.description: The ex-Oldham MP Phil Woolas battled brain cancer for more than a year, his family says.

item.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
item.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

item.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
item.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

item.title: More US Marines and warships being moved to Middle East, reports say
item.description: The reinforcements will come from an amphibious ready group that is usually based in Japan, an official told CBS, the BBC's US partner.

item.title: Where are the Houthis?
item.description: Yemeni militant group is noticeably absent from Iran’s war with the US and Israel

item.title: Why has Trump left Iran’s nuclear stockpile untouched?
item.description: US administration appears to have no plan to retrieve the enriched uranium after two weeks of military action

item.title: Netanyahu Has the War He Always Wanted, but on Trump’s Terms
item.description: Israel’s prime minister wanted regime change in Iran. But President Trump seems prepared to settle for something less.

item.title: Mahmood Mamdani, Father of NYC Mayor, Is Writing a Book on Gaza
item.description: At once, Mahmood Mamdani’s fame was eclipsed by his son’s. At the same time, the election of Zohran Mamdani has attracted new interest in his father’s work.

item.title: His Film Is Spain’s Submission to the Oscars. He’s Not Sure How Spanish It Is.
item.description: Spanish cinema has entered a new and more diverse era, film experts say. Oliver Laxe, the director of Oscar-nominated “Sirat,” embodies the shift.

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s Holy War
item.description: Christian nationalism was once a fringe ideology in America. The Secretary of War has ushered it into the heart of U.S. military force.

item.title: Chancellor to offer support over rising heating oil costs
item.description: Rachel Reeves says the Treasury is also looking at "different options" to help households most vulnerable to soaring energy bills.

item.title: Doomed hereditary peers spy chance to stay in the Lords
item.description: The Tories are deciding which hereditary peers to keep after being offered 15 seats in a compromise deal.

item.title: Fears for press freedom as billionaire takes control of East Africa's largest media house
item.description: Rostam Aziz's purchase raises questions about continued journalistic independence in the region.

item.title: 'Tigers and flies': Millions of officials later, why is Xi's corruption purge still going?
item.description: Critics say Xi's purges reveal a ruthless drive for absolute loyalty and total control of the military.

item.title: Ethics adviser rejects Tory call for inquiry into PM over Mandelson appointment
item.description: Sir Laurie Magnus said the relevant process appeared to have been followed when Lord Mandelson was made ambassador.

item.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
item.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

item.title: Cuba confirms negotiations with US as country faces effects of oil blockade
item.description: Cuba's president confirmed the talks in a national broadcast as the country also said it would release 51 prisoners in "goodwill" gesture.

item.title: Tourism was booming in Cyprus, then a drone hit
item.description: 2025 was a record-breaking year for Cyprus' tourist industry, with more than 4.5 million visitors but since the Middle East conflict numbers have dropped.

item.title: Trial starts in case of explosives sent to UK and Poland
item.description: The parcel plot is one of a wave of sabotage attacks across Europe which officials have linked to Russia.

item.title: Gulf states are burning through interceptors
item.description: The pace far outstrips that seen in Ukraine. Stocks are under severe pressure

item.title: Austrian glaciers disintegrating due to climate change, say scientists
item.description: Scientists say nearly all of the significant glaciers in the Austrian Alps have shrunk.

item.title: First image emerges of Andrew, Mandelson and Epstein together
item.description: The image shows Lord Mandelson and the former prince in bathrobes sitting on wooden decking with Epstein.

item.title: Petrol retailers in row with government over 'rip off' accusations
item.description: They briefly threatened to pull out of a government meeting, accusing ministers of using "inflammatory language" over rising fuel prices.

item.title: UK economy flatlines in January as people cut back on eating out
item.description: Analysts had been expecting 0.2% growth for the UK economy at the beginning of the year.

item.title: Ukraine and allies fear easing Russian sanctions will prolong war
item.description: Ukraine's president says the US decision "certainly does not help achieve peace".

item.title: Why has Trump eased sanctions on Russian oil - and will it help Putin?
item.description: The US said easing sanctions on Russian oil would provide only a limited financial boost to Putin.

item.title: France returns sacred 'talking drum' looted during colonial rule to Ivory Coast
item.description: The move forms part of a broader process of returning cultural artefacts to African countries that started in 2017.‎

item.title: Iranians react to new supreme leader's first address
item.description: People in Iran are questioning who's really in charge after Mojtaba Khamenei's first address came via a presenter on state TV.

item.title: Iran war increases importance of good UK-Ireland relations
item.description: The second summit follows the announcement of new Irish investment of more than £900m into the UK.

item.title: UK urges allies to keep up pressure on Russia as US eases sanctions
item.description: Ministers say the UK will not be following the US by loosening sanctions on some Russian oil.

item.title: All six crew members killed after US refuelling plane crashes in Iraq
item.description: The US Central Command says all six crew members died after a refuelling aircraft went down over western Iraq.

item.title: Games with loot boxes to get minimum 16 age rating across Europe
item.description: The new changes by the Pan-European Game Information age-ratings body (PEGI) will start from June.

item.title: Farage regrets taking control of 'bankrupt' council
item.description: The Reform UK leader says he wishes the party "hadn't bothered" to take control in Worcestershire.

item.title: Ghana star to deny rape charges, UK court hears
item.description: Partey is to appear at crown court next month, charged with raping a woman in December 2020.

item.title: 'No-one will hire women' - India's top court rejects menstrual leave petition
item.description: The subject has polarised India for long, though some states and private companies offer menstrual leaves.

item.title: Bodies of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US torpedo strike to be repatriated
item.description: The seamen died when Iranian warship the Iris Dena was sunk on 4 March by a US submarine near Sri Lanka.

item.title: Minister in Belgium amid concern over migrant boats
item.description: The visit comes as people smugglers target the Belgian coast, away from main launch sites in France.

item.title: Post Office scandal 'has taken 21 years of my life'
item.description: Seema Misra calls for accountability as a report by MPs raises concerns about ongoing delays.

item.title: Australian businessman found guilty of working for suspected Chinese spies
item.description: Csergo argued his reports did not contain any secrets but only information available on the internet.

item.title: South Korea police raid transport ministry over Jeju Air crash
item.description: More inquiries into the incident have been launched, as investigators discover more body parts and victims' belongings.

item.title: The US may move some of its anti-missile system - and it's sparking unease in South Korea
item.description: Reports say the US may be moving parts of its Thaad system to the Middle East, despite Seoul's opposition.

item.title: KPop Demon Hunters to return as Netflix announces sequel
item.description: The sequel brings back the co-directors of the first film, which was a smash-hit for the streaming service.

item.title: Churchill's granddaughter happy with his picture being replaced on £5 note
item.description: Emma Soames rejected Nigel Farage's statement that the currency change was "wokery".

item.title: 'There's no hiding place on a ship': The sailors stranded near Iran
item.description: Drones, cruise missiles and fighter jets have become a common sight for many sailors stranded on ships in the Gulf.

item.title: Qantas agrees to pay $74m over Covid-19 travel voucher refunds
item.description: The case relates to cancelled flights during the pandemic, for which customers were given credits instead of cash.

item.title: French vote tests polarised electorate with right hoping to win control of Paris
item.description: The highest-profile contest is for the mayorship of Paris - which has been under left-wing control for 25 years.

item.title: Why Donald Trump Doesn’t Want to Talk About the War in Iran
item.description: “We won,” the President who’s treating the conflict with Iran like a video game says, but “we’re not finished yet.”

item.title: Israel pounds Beirut suburbs and south Lebanon after Hezbollah rocket barrage
item.description: Some Israeli strikes also hit other parts of the Lebanese capital, with 12 people reportedly killed along the seafront.

item.title: 'Follow the smoke': BBC spends day with emergency teams as Israel strikes south Lebanon
item.description: The BBC visited the city of Nabatieh, witnessing the pace and scale of Israel's bombing as its war with Hezbollah continues to escalate.

item.title: Israeli military drops charges against soldiers accused of abusing Palestinian detainee
item.description: The Israeli military's top lawyer cites "exceptional circumstances" for dropping the case involving a Palestinian man held at Sde Teiman military prison.

item.title: Iran's new supreme leader vows to block Strait of Hormuz in first statement
item.description: A message attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei also says Iran will continue targeting US bases in the region.

item.title: PM says Mandelson appointment was mistake as No 10 denies cover-up
item.description: Documents show Sir Keir Starmer was warned the peer's relationship with Jeffrey Epstein posed a "reputational risk".

item.title: Emirati minister tells BBC Iran must end strikes on Gulf
item.description: The UAE says more than 1,800 drones and missiles have been projected at the country since the war began.

item.title: Watchdog says he 'can't trust ministers' with Sturgeon inquiry files
item.description: The information commissioner has threatened the government with more legal action over the release of files.

item.title: I can survive no-confidence vote - council leader
item.description: Reform UK's George Finch says he is confident he will stay in charge on Warwickshire County Council.

item.title: Globetrotting Constantine back in charge of Rwanda
item.description: Rwanda reappoint globetrotting Englishman Stephen Constantine as head coach of their men's national team on a two-year contract.

item.title: Tracking the wave of ship attacks that has choked off Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Six ships have been reportedly attacked in the Gulf in less than 48 hours, bringing the total vessels attacked during the war to 18.

item.title: British man charged in Dubai for alleged filming of Iranian missiles
item.description: Foreign Office officials are understood to be supporting his family.

item.title: Parents accused of hiding daughter's HIV infection and neglect
item.description: The child did not see a doctor until she was six - and had not had any vaccinations, Italian media say.

item.title: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah's party wins Nepal election
item.description: The Rastriya Swatatantra Party has won a large majority in an election dominated by issues including corruption.

item.title: A popular German Green wins a surprise victory
item.description: Cem Özdemir becomes the first Turkish-German to run a state

item.title: In Paris’s mayoral race, it’s drivers against cyclists
item.description: A decade of greening leaves the capital less congested but more divided

item.title: As war rages, Turkey’s strongman puts the opposition on trial
item.description: Chaos in the Middle East makes President Erdogan indispensable to the West

item.title: Kenya’s ailing sugar sector is a test case for reform
item.description: Low productivity and political corruption bedevil the whole economy

item.title: What data reveal about the war’s progress
item.description: We analyse 1,615 distinct attacks, and more besides

item.title: Iran’s praetorian guard may leave the war diminished but undefeated
item.description: Even if it will struggle to rebuild its capabilities

item.title: Who is Iran’s new leader?
item.description: No one knows if he will last

item.title: Islamists woo Bangladesh with everything but Islam
item.description: They are a hit on campus, too

item.title: Taiwan’s bid to export drones free of Chinese parts is taking off
item.description: The island’s unmanned aerial vehicles are already in use on Ukraine’s front line

item.title: Nepal’s new prime minister is a 35-year-old former rapper
item.description: Voters have given Balendra Shah an unprecedented mandate and enormous power

item.title: Seoul’s housing market is a huge political and economic headache
item.description: Lee Jae Myung is staking his presidency on fixing it

item.title: Brazilian cinema is having its moment
item.description: Thank growing competition and a more internationally minded Academy awards

item.title: China’s nationalist spy thriller has few girls and lots of government
item.description: “Main melody” films are in a rut

item.title: Hong Kong’s property market has turned
item.description: That’s not good news for everyone

item.title: Some of China’s officials are becoming social-media stars
item.description: It may help them get promotions

item.title: Haiti needs order first, then elections
item.description: Voters must be able to turn out without risking death

item.title: How to teach Donald Trump a Latin lesson
item.description: By alienating Hispanics, he has given Democrats an open goal

item.title: China’s hereditary elite is taking shape
item.description: The Communist Party is afraid to tax inherited wealth

item.title: How Iran war laid bare the world's reliance on Gulf oil and gas
item.description: Countries around the world are feeling the impact of the conflict and the resulting energy price shock.

item.title: Cartoonist freed after 15 years in prison without charge in Eritrea
item.description: Biniam Solomon was known for his witty and critical cartoons before the free press was closed.

item.title: Nineteen jailed over deadly Moscow concert attack
item.description: Gunmen opened fire at Crocus City Concert hall near Moscow in 2024, killing 149 and injuring more than 500.

item.title: 'What if we're left with ruins?': Doubts creep in for Iranians who supported war
item.description: Teachers, engineers and shopkeepers tell the BBC they fear Iran falling into chaos.

item.title: Questions mount for Hegseth over possible US involvement in strike on Iranian school
item.description: Democrats have written to ask the defence secretary whether the US was responsible, but he says the issue is being investigated.

item.title: Polanski stood by breast enlargement hypnosis claim
item.description: A newly unearthed interview from 2013 casts doubt on the Green Party leader's claim to have apologised.

item.title: An attack on the world economy
item.description: Whatever happens in the Strait of Hormuz, energy markets have been changed for ever

item.title: There are no good options for Iran’s nuclear programme
item.description: If America cannot eliminate the threat, what should it do?

item.title: 'Fingers on the trigger': Deadly warnings for Iranians being urged to take action
item.description: Iran's police chief has warned that his forces would treat anyone taking to the streets "at the enemy's request" as an enemy.
